ground_truth.title: Deploy failed for unknown reason
ground_truth.author: tinterweb
ground_truth.main_content:
tinterweb
April 26, 2024, 6:28am

Subject: Deploy failed for unknown reason

All of a suddent the deployment for the following site https://karinagrantmarketing.netlify.app/ has stopped deploying, the build passes but when it gets to the deployment stage it shows

6:58:36 AM: Deploy site
6:58:36 AM: ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6:58:36 AM:
6:58:36 AM: Starting to deploy site from 'gatsby/public'
6:58:37 AM: Calculating files to upload
6:58:37 AM: Execution cancelled
6:58:37 AM: Failing build: Failed to build site

A bit of past history as we saw this error here back in February

When this happened in February I changed the node version in this (AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME) envionrment variable.

I thought this had fixed the issue as it then started to build.

The issue came back and now I am a loss of what could be causing it, maybe a timeout or size of the build is too big.

Here is the build log which passes.

6:51:42 AM: Netlify Build
6:51:42 AM: ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6:51:42 AM: Version
6:51:42 AM: @netlify/build 29.41.1
6:51:42 AM: Flags
6:51:42 AM: baseRelDir: true
6:51:42 AM: buildId: 662b40a08b83d4726825275b
6:51:42 AM: deployId: 662b40a08b83d4726825275d
6:51:42 AM: Current directory
6:51:42 AM: /opt/build/repo/gatsby
6:51:42 AM: Config file
6:51:42 AM: /opt/build/repo/gatsby/netlify.toml
6:51:42 AM: Context
6:51:42 AM: production

Please can anyone shed any light on what may be causing it?

Thanks in advance for any help you can offer here. We are at a loss and are unable to make any changes to the site.

nathanmartin
April 26, 2024, 7:23am

I'm confused.

If I'm reading your post correctly, you've mostly supplied a portion of a previous build log where the build succeeded? But only the "end result message" of the one that failed?

tinterweb
April 26, 2024, 12:09pm

Hi @nathanmartin

Apologies i thought I added this. Here is the deploy message

6:58:36 AM: Deploy site
6:58:36 AM: ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6:58:36 AM:
6:58:36 AM: Starting to deploy site from 'gatsby/public'
6:58:37 AM: Calculating files to upload
6:58:37 AM: Execution cancelled
6:58:37 AM: Failing build: Failed to build site

Thanks for your help.
Simon

nathanmartin
April 26, 2024, 12:42pm

@tinterweb That's effectively only the end result, it doesn't reveal any useful indication of "why it happened".

That information may be elsewhere in the log.

It's why the instructions for the forum indicate you should provide full build logs.

tinterweb
April 26, 2024, 1:52pm

Thanks @nathanmartin below is the full log.

There is nothing useful at all that tells me what's causing the failed deploy.

When this started all that was changed was a template change. No npm updates or node version changes. I have tried deploying and clearing the cache but nothing.

Could it be the build is too large?

sid.m
April 26, 2024, 6:19pm

The build is failing because you're using an unsupported version of node for functions - please refer to this guide for how to upgrade that version:

End of support for Node.js 16 in Netlify Functions: Everything you need to know

Node.js version 16 reached End of Life (EOL) on September 11, 2023. Netlify Functions will stop supporting serverless function deploys using Node.js 16 on April 25, 2024.

Who is impacted?
You will be impacted if you have explicitly set the AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME environment variable to nodejs16.x.
If you keep the environment variable set to nodejs16.x and deploy any changes to your existing functions on or after April 25, 2024, your build will fail.

tinterweb
April 30, 2024, 5:09am

Thanks @sid.m this fixed it.
with: All of a suddent the deployment for the following site https://karinagrantmarketing.netlify.app/ has stopped deploying
When this happened in February I changed the node version in this (AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME) envionrment variable.
If I'm reading your post correctly, you've mostly supplied a portion of a previous build log where the build succeeded?
That's effectively only the end result, it doesn't reveal any useful indication of "why it happened".
The build is failing because you're using an unsupported version of node for functions
Node.js version 16 reached End of Life (EOL) on September 11, 2023.
